title: pittosporum dallii
intro: Pittosporum dallii, also known as Kohuhu, is a small evergreen tree native to New Zealand, belonging to the Pittosporaceae family. The leaves and bark of this plant have been traditionally used in herbal medicine, exhibiting antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties. The medicinal properties of Pittosporum dallii are said to provide various health benefits, including relief from pain, fever reduction, and wound healing. In herbal preparations, the leaves and bark are often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, although further research is needed to confirm the effectiveness and safety of these uses.
